purpose: The commit fixes queuing commands when HCI_UNREGISTER is set, preventing a use-after-free bug by returning an error.
Removes an unnecessary header include to clean up code dependencies.
The commit updates documentation for a 'N/A' store metric in perf c2c, reflecting changes in report tables.

The commit fixes race conditions in hugetlb_unmap_file_folio and ensures proper vma lock handling during vma splitting. It addresses issues with VM_MAYSHARE flags and prevents double freeing of locks.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply documentation to Realtek rt5682s codec bindings.
Fixes a resource leak by adding missing iounmap() in remove function.
Removes an unused 'work' field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx, cleaning up the code.
The commit changes the key_idx struct member to signed to prevent static checker warnings when -1 is assigned.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs without any functional change.
Refactors CAN-FD mode check by introducing mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() function.
The commit modifies self-tests to skip RTC tests when the hardware isn't present, preventing unnecessary failures.
Removes redundant assignment to 'bytes' variable and fixes a dead code warning.
Decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to reduce kernel size and complexity.
Removes duplicate 'be split' text in spte.h, improving code readability without changing functionality.
Reverts populating DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK due to causing read issues when states are disabled.
Adds device tree binding documentation for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC with Mali-G31 GPU.
Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for naming consistency within the DW PCIe core driver.
Fixes a Clang compiler warning about incorrect format specifier by changing the format from '%#06hx' to '%#06x', ensuring proper type promotion and preventing potential issues.
Fixes a bug in MIPS jump_label by adjusting branch range check to prevent warnings from negative offsets.
Removes HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() and calls function directly, part of removing unwanted HAL layer.
The commit changes non-global symbols to static in arm64 code to fix sparse warnings, improving code quality.
Removes incorrect interrupt flags specification in device tree bindings for MDSS display nodes.
The commit allocates arrays in struct ctrl_ref to allow drivers to change array sizes, improving flexibility.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver, enhancing hardware recognition.
The commit updates the WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (versions A-P), improving reporting and handling of different hardware versions.
Removes redundant .is_enabled state from PRCMU clocks as core already tracks enable count.
The commit refactors RISC-V memory management initialization code to reduce the use of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by grouping similar cases together.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware on sm6350-lena to support future I2C nodes.
This commit improves code comments by clarifying descriptions, fixing a typo, and correcting coding style.
Fixes an infinite loop caused by failed HW semaphore acquisition when hardware is removed.
Updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI terminology, improving code readability.
Removes an unnecessary code check for IS_SWAPFILE since it's always defined.
Implements GTP echo response functionality to comply with specifications by sending appropriate responses to Echo Requests.
Replaces cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* for efficiency and creates cpumask_first_zero().
Removes an unsupported firmware call causing console errors during roaming.
Adds board config adl_rt5682 for alc5682 codec without amplifier, connecting to SSP0.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail when unsupported by the socket, preventing incorrect behavior.
The commit adds a check for xeCJK.sty before loading it in the kernel documentation to prevent build errors on systems where the package is missing.
The commit modifies checkpatch script to allow 'case' macros without errors, preventing false positives in code checks.
Refactor simpledrm to use atomic helpers for better code architecture and maintainability.
Adds jpeg decode worker interface to enable parallel HW operation in MT8195.
Removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and a function, replacing them with tracepoints for better debugging.
The commit adds support for entering a low-power zstate during extended vblank periods in the AMD display driver, optimizing power management.
Fixes type mismatch in hqd_destroy() callback causing CFI failure.
Corrects 'resets' property name in device tree bindings and adds missing validation to prevent incorrect usage.
Silences an array-bounds warning in ath9k driver by adjusting compiler warning level.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in sun8i-ss crypto driver to simplify code.
Refactor code by moving fixed file installation/removal into filetable section and modifying helpers to take ctx instead of io_kiocb for better context handling.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes, reflecting their independence and separate power-domain control.
Removes an unused macro to simplify code structure.
